seo.title: E-Commerce tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of E-Commerce tools and references.

content.intro: A curated directory of tools, platforms, and services for developers building modern, headless e-commerce architectures, focusing on performance, scalability, and developer experience.

entries.name: Medusa
entries.description: An open-source Node.js commerce engine designed for modularity and extensibility via a plugin-based architecture.
pros: Highly extensible through custom plugins
Self-hostable for full data control
Clear documentation for backend customization
cons: Requires more infrastructure management than SaaS
Smaller ecosystem of pre-built integrations

entries.name: Shopify Hydrogen
entries.description: A React-based framework for building custom, performant storefronts on the Shopify platform using Remix.
pros: Native integration with Shopify Storefront API
Built-in edge caching and streaming SSR
Optimized for high-performance product pages
cons: Vendor lock-in to Shopify ecosystem
Best performance requires Oxygen hosting

entries.name: Saleor
entries.description: A GraphQL-first headless commerce platform built with Python/Django, offering robust multi-channel capabilities.
pros: Comprehensive GraphQL API for all operations
Native support for multi-warehouse and multi-currency
Clean, modern dashboard for store management
cons: Steep learning curve for developers new to GraphQL
Complex local setup for development

entries.name: Stripe
entries.description: Financial infrastructure platform providing APIs for payment processing, subscription management, and global tax compliance.
pros: Industry-standard documentation and SDKs
Pre-built checkout components and elements
Handles complex global tax and compliance automatically
cons: Transaction fees can be high for low-margin businesses
Account freezes can occur without warning during disputes

entries.name: Algolia
entries.description: A hosted search and discovery API that provides sub-millisecond search results and AI-powered recommendations.
pros: Extremely fast search response times
Robust UI libraries for React, Vue, and JavaScript
Powerful merchandising and ranking controls
cons: Costs scale rapidly with record count and search volume
Configuration complexity for multi-language search

entries.name: Cloudinary
entries.description: Cloud-based image and video management service that automates media optimization and delivery via URL parameters.
pros: Automatic format selection (WebP/AVIF) based on browser
On-the-fly resizing and cropping via URL
Global CDN for low-latency asset delivery
cons: Management console is complex and cluttered
Bandwidth costs can be unpredictable

entries.name: Sanity.io
entries.description: A platform for structured content that treats content as data, enabling real-time collaboration and custom editing environments.
pros: Highly customizable 'Studio' using React
Powerful GROQ query language for complex data fetching
Real-time multi-user editing and versioning
cons: Requires significant developer setup time
GROQ has a learning curve compared to REST

entries.name: Meilisearch
entries.description: An open-source, lightning-fast search engine designed to be a simpler, self-hosted alternative to Algolia.
pros: Easy to install and maintain
Excellent typo tolerance out of the box
No complex configuration required for basic use
cons: Lacks some advanced AI features found in Algolia
Memory usage can be high for very large datasets

entries.name: TaxJar
entries.description: Automated sales tax calculation, reporting, and filing service specifically for e-commerce businesses.
pros: Real-time tax calculation at checkout
Automated nexus tracking across US states
Simplifies multi-state tax filing
cons: Pricing is based on transaction volume
Primarily focused on the US market

entries.name: Sentry
entries.description: Application monitoring and error tracking tool that helps developers identify and fix checkout and performance issues.
pros: Session replay to see exactly where users fail in checkout
Detailed stack traces for frontend and backend errors
Performance profiling for slow API calls
cons: Can be noisy if alerts are not properly filtered
Data retention limits on free tier
